element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational - player defines race, gender, class, birthsign through dialogue with NPCs (fellow prisoner, officer, bureaucrat)
Learning through practice - weapon skills via combat, armor skills via taking damage, other skills via specific actions or training NPCs
Three attack types (chop, slash, thrust) learned through trial; hidden arithmetic modifiers determine hit success
Player gains levels in ten pre-determined major/minor skills; each level-up allows attribute augmentation tied to skill performance
Open-ended design allows players to pursue central quest or ignore in favor of freeform exploration

typical_tension_triggers: Venturing into unexplored wilderness or dungeons
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear and player-paced; freeform design emphasizes exploration over mandatory progression, allowing tension cycles to be self-determined rather than scripted

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Wilderness and dungeon exploration available beyond main quest path; player agency in choosing what areas to visit

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through character creation and tutorial boat sequence; initial combat encounters manageable with proper skill selection
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalates as player ventures into more dangerous wilderness areas and pursues deeper dungeon content
difficulty_curve.late_game: Open-ended design means difficulty depends on player choices; central quest (Dagoth Ur/Red Mountain) represents endgame challenge if pursued
notable_spikes: Transitioning from tutorial to open world exploration
Encountering unexpected dangerous enemies in wilderness
Pursuing main quest without adequate preparation

notable_level_patterns.description: Game begins with scripted prisoner boat arrival and character creation dialogue sequence before releasing player into explorable Vvardenfell island setting
Main plot available but optional; players can pursue central Dagoth Ur questline or engage in side quests, exploration, and character development independently
Player improves specific skills by performing associated actions (weapon skills via strikes, armor skills via damage taken, etc.) creating organic learning curve tied to playstyle
